senses.definition: a person who creates a new version of something or recovers something in a new form; one who reinvents
examples: The inventor became known as a reinventor when she transformed an old idea into a modern gadget.
As a reinventor, he reimagined the company’s product line to fit sustainable trends.

senses.definition: to make a new version of something or change it so it is different from before
examples: She reinvents her career every few years.
The company reinvents its product line to stay competitive.

senses.definition: the act or process of turning something back to its previous form or state; a reversion with a new form
examples: The reinversion of the theory required careful study.
Scholars discussed reinversion as a bridge between old and new ideas.

senses.definition: to invert again or turn something back to its original orientation or state
examples: They decided to reinvert the design after user feedback.
If the process fails, reinvert the changes to the previous version.

senses.definition: to invest money again, typically into the same project or venture
examples: The profits were reinvested into expanding the business.
They decided to reinvest in new equipment.

senses.definition: past tense of reinvest; invested again into the same or a new project
examples: They reinvested their dividends into the fund.
The revenue was reinvested to fuel growth.

senses.definition: to investigate again, often with new evidence or methods
examples: The committee decided to reinvestigate the case.
Experts were asked to reinvestigate the findings with fresh data.

senses.definition: past tense of reinvestigate; investigated again
examples: Officials reinvestigated the incident after new footage appeared.
The report was reinvestigated and amended.

senses.definition: third person singular present of reinvestigate; investigates again
examples: The committee reinvestigates the case when new information emerges.
Researchers reinvestigates discrepancies in the data.

senses.definition: present participle form; in the process of investigating again
examples: They are reinvestigating the evidence to ensure accuracy.
Reinvestigating the case could uncover new information.

senses.definition: the act of investigating again or a second investigation
examples: The reinvestigation concluded with new findings.
A reinvestigation was requested by the defense.

senses.definition: plural of reinvestigation; multiple second investigations
examples: Several reinvestigations were requested over the years.
The team reviewed the reinvestigations for consistency.

senses.definition: present participle of reinvest; investing again
examples: They are reinvesting profits to grow the business.
Reinvesting in education can benefit a community long-term.

senses.definition: the act of investing someone again or bestowing a ceremonial symbol of office again
examples: The reinvestiture ceremony restored the ceremonial duties.
Historically, reinvestiture marked the return of powers.

senses.definition: the act of putting money or profits back into something, such as a project or fund
examples: Reinvestment of profits supports growth.
Tax rules affect reinvestment decisions.

senses.definition: plural of reinvestment; profits or funds reinvested
examples: The company made several reinvestments into new technology.
Reinvestments helped stabilize the portfolio.

senses.definition: third person singular present of reinvest; invests again
examples: The fund reinvests all gains back into the market.
She reinvests part of her salary each month.

senses.definition: to give new energy or strength to something
examples: A fresh campaign can reinvigorate public interest.
The team worked to reinvigorate the economy.

senses.definition: past tense of reinvigorate; given new energy
examples: The project was reinvigorated by new leadership.
After the break, the team felt reinvigorated.

senses.definition: third person singular present of reinvigorate; gives new energy
examples: New ideas reinvigorates the team's motivation.
Advertising reinvigorates interest in the product.

senses.definition: present participle; in the process of giving new energy
examples: The campaign is reinvigorating public support.
They're reinvigorating the brand with a bold redesign.

senses.definition: the act or process of giving new energy or vitality
examples: The reinvigoration of the city attracted new investment.
There was a reinvigoration of interest in the arts.

senses.definition: plural of reinvigoration; multiple instances of renewed energy
examples: Several reinvigoration efforts followed the policy changes.
The reinvigortions in the marketplace were evident.

senses.definition: a person or thing that reinvigorates or restores energy
examples: The new manager served as a reinvigorator for the team.
The event acted as a reinvigorator for the local economy.

senses.definition: plural of reinvigorator; agents that reinvigorate
examples: New policies are among the reinvigorators of growth.
Several reinvigorators helped attract investment.
